turns.text: yeah so Julie actually has been someone who been struggling with headaches for years now ever since Julie was a teenager and the headaches used to be around Julie's when Julie would have Julie's menstrual Julie's menses but as Julie got older Julie noticed that the headaches has been with that and also Julie can get the headaches when Julie's really stressed but recently and why Julie went to Julie's PCP is because Julie's headaches have been getting a lot more the headaches have just been happening a lot more frequently and a lot more severe

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay can the doctor tell Julie a little bit about the headaches what how do the headaches how do the headaches feel when the headaches come on

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so the headaches stay mostly on Julie's right side and the headaches even seems like the headaches are like behind Julie's eye and the headaches are just like this pulsing like this really bad pulsing sensation

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay does Julie notice any activities or time of the day that makes the headaches worse the doctor knows Julie said the headaches seem to get worse around Julie's menstrual periods but is there any time of the day or that makes the headaches worse

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: Julie have noticed that you know Julie's been trying to exercise more in order to lose weight but Julie ca n't even really exercise for very long because Julie start of as soon as Julie start to jog Julie's headaches the headaches come back on a lot worse so Julie notice the headaches when Julie's exercising

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay and does Julie seem to have any other symptoms when Julie get these headaches the doctor has been having a ringing in the doctor's ears for a few months now and then what really scared the doctor is that now the doctor is starting to get some blurred vision the blurred vision is not all the time but the blurred vision will come and go but some blurred like on the

turns.speaker: D

turns.text: no Julie have n't

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay so Julie Julie like to run a lot what kind of do Julie do does Julie use a a good shoe you know what kind of shoes does Julie use for Julie's running

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: yeah Julie yeah Julie is starting to get back into running Julie's a big new balance person in asics so yeah those are the two brands Julie like to wear yeah so right now Julie's using asics

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay yeah the doctor like to run too and the doctor use nike

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: D

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: so has Julie had any let's get back to the exam here has Julie had a recent mri of Julie's brain the doctor saw one from a few years ago but as doctor white said anything about getting a more a recent mri

turns.speaker: D

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay so that's good the doctor think the doctor will the doctor will follow up with that mri but let's go ahead and take a do a quick physical exam here the doctor reviewed Julie's vitals that the assistant collected when Julie came in and everything there looks good including Julie's blood pressure you know with symptoms like the symptoms that's one of those things that the doctor'd be concerned about

turns.speaker: D

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: now on Julie's heart exam Julie's heart exam sounds like Julie's heart exam is normal sinus rhythm with no murmurs rubs or gallops so that's good as the doctor listen the doctor'm gon na go ahead and look here in Julie's eyes the funduscopic exam shows papilledema and papilledema is just some of swelling behind there in the eye and this let the doctor do a a neurological exam this neurological exam will take a few minutes Julie get up to follow the doctor's instructions as the doctor go through that okay on neurological exam Julie's cranial nerves one through twelve are intact including a normal cranial reflex and just went to watch Julie walk across the room here

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay turn around come back and okay so on musculoskeletal exam Julie's gait is normal okay go ahead sorry

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: that's okay for Julie's first problem Julie's headaches Julie's symptoms are concerning for what the doctor call idiopathic intracranial hypertension and idiopathic intracranial hypertension is a condition that that Julie have increased pressure in the in Julie's head in Julie's brain without any known cause for the increased pressure now patient also presents with often present with symptoms such as Julie's you know headaches worsening with activity ringing in the ears changes in Julie's vision so the doctor'm pretty confident that idiopathic intracranial hypertension is what the doctor're having here now why the doctor get concerned is if the pressure continues to increase the pressure can lead to some permanent changes in Julie's vision so the doctor do wan na get a a few more tests just so the doctor can confirm that diagnosis and the first test that the doctor just talked about is an mri the doctor think the MRI is really important to get the MRI to check for any other condition that could be contributing to Julie's symptoms now once the doctor have the MRI result the doctor'm gon na be doing also doing a spinal tap which will help the doctor evaluate the pressure in the brain and the spinal tap can also help Julie feel better if Julie do have an increased pressure by taking the decreasing the amount of the increased pressure just by taking a a a little bit of fluid off of the increased pressure and lastly the doctor want Julie to be seen by a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam now the doctor know that sounds like a lot of information but the doctor think the doctor is really important the doctor get the MRI, the spinal tap, and the neuro-ophthalmologist visit accomplished does Julie have any questions for the doctor

turns.speaker: D

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: not necessarily if Julie have idiopathic intracranial hypertension there are number of medications that the doctor can try to help decrease the pressure in Julie's brain and the medications will also help Julie have less headaches when Julie exercise as exercise and weight loss are also an important part of the treatment

Patient is starting to get back into running.
Patient reports headaches come back a lot worse when exercising/jogging and cannot exercise for very long.
Speaker states they use Nike and switched over to Nike from another brand.
